svg.text: Tracing one scenario: tacos run out

g.text: Customer orders 2 street tacos
POST /order → shop agent processes via handle_customer()
Inventory decrements: tacos 7 → 5
_decrement_inventory() — pure Python, no LLM call
Threshold hit: 5 remaining = restock_threshold
check_autonomous_triggers() fires automatically after every order
LLM decides what to do (1 API call)
"Tacos are low (5 left). Restock costs $30. You have $470 cash."
→ Returns: {"action": "restock", "quantity": 20, "reason": "..."}
Action applied to state
_apply_action(): tacos 5 → 25, cash $470 → $440
Response sent back to frontend (one JSON payload)
{ cashier_message, order, autonomous_actions[], updated_state }
React updates: chat, inventory bars, action feed, revenue

svg.text: Alternate scenario: what if cash is too low?

g.text: LLM decides differently with low cash
"Tacos low (5 left). Restock costs $30. You only have $15 cash."
→ Returns: {"action": "adjust_price", "new_price": 5.50, "reason": "..."}
Different state change, same frontend flow
Price display updates, action feed shows "raised taco price to $5.50
to slow demand and preserve remaining stock"
